title: Sphaeralcea elegans
intro: Sphaeralcea elegans, also known as the Desert Marigold, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as soothing digestive issues and treating skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Desert Marigold include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for skin care.
taxonomy: Sphaeralcea elegans is classified within the family Malvaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Sphaeralcea, which comprises about 14 species of flowering plants. Sphaeralcea elegans is further classified as a perennial or annual herb, depending on the region. The species is native to the southwestern United States, particularly in the deserts of Arizona and California. It is often referred to as the desert marigold due to its bright yellow and orange flowers.
uses: Sphaeralcea elegans, also known as the elegant globemallow, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes.
